name: Other soy product (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Other soy product [Soy] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: PHENOL EXPLORER, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 174273 (Soy flour, full-fat, raw) - full macro/mineral/vitamin/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel
Jang et al. 2021, Foods, PMC8468453 - individual free sugars (sucrose/stachyose/raffinose/glucose/fructose/galactose) and isoflavones of soybean
Kim et al. 2020, Food Prod Process Nutr, doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1 - soybean organic-acid profile (malic dominant)
SNI Global oxalate review / soybean cultivar survey - oxalate 82.3-213.6 mg/100g
Soybean phytate composition literature - phytic acid ~2.3 g/100g
provenance.curation_notes: 'Other soy product' is a generic FooDB category with no single defining recipe; quantified here against full-fat soy flour (USDA FDC 174273) as a representative non-fermented soy product. Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe are the free/oligo sugars - sucrose (~4.5 g) plus the alpha-galactosides stachyose (~2.5 g) and raffinose (~0.8 g) - together with abundant amino acids from ~38% protein. Phytic acid (~2.3 g) is the dominant organic acid and a strong mineral chelator affecting Ca/Fe/
curation_added: sucrose, stachyose, raffinose, glucose, fructose, galactose (individual soy sugars with amounts; oligosaccharides stachyose+raffinose are the defining soy carbohydrates)
phytic acid (dominant soy organic acid / mineral chelator)
oxalic acid, malic acid, citric acid, succinic acid, fumaric acid (soy organic acids)
L-valine and L-phenylalanine (missing essential amino acids)
magnesium, phosphorus, potassium, sodium, zinc (quantified minerals absent as numbers before)
thiamine, riboflavin, niacin, B6, folate, vitamin E, vitamin K, choline amounts (quantified)
oleic acid, palmitic acid, stearic acid (fatty acid detail)

curation_removed.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
curation_removed.reason: EPA (20:5 n-3) is a marine/animal long-chain omega-3; soy oil contains NO EPA (only 18:2 and 18:3). FooDB predicted/implausible for a plant food.

curation_removed.name: Heptanoate
curation_removed.reason: Heptanoic acid (C7 odd-chain) is not a soy nutrient; FooDB noise, not a real growth substrate of soy.

curation_removed.name: Retinol
curation_removed.reason: Preformed vitamin A (animal form) is absent in soy; the food supplies provitamin-A carotenoids (beta-carotene) only. Replaced by 'vitamin A (as beta-carotene)'.

curation_removed.name: Cyanocobalamin
curation_removed.reason: Vitamin B12 is absent in non-fermented plant soy (USDA B12 = 0); synthetic cyanocobalamin is not a native soy compound. Would only apply to fermented soy (natto/tempeh) or fortified products.

curation_removed.name: D-Serine
curation_removed.reason: Proteinogenic serine is the L enantiomer; 'D-Serine' is a database enantiomer artifact. Replaced by 'L-serine'.

curation_removed.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria (duplicate iron entries)
curation_removed.reason: Redundant oxidation-state/compartment splits of one measured element; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry (6.4 mg/100g).

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 174273 (total sugars 7.5 g) apportioned by soybean sugar profile in Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453

components.name: stachyose
components.evidence_source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (soybean seed 1.4-4.1%, up to 28.2 mg/g)

components.name: raffinose
components.evidence_source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (soybean seed 0.1-1.0%, up to 9.3 mg/g)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence_source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (~6 mg/g max in seed)

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence_source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (~2.7 mg/g max in seed)

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence_source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (1.4-2.5 mg/g in seed)

components.name: starch
components.evidence_source: Mature soybean composition (starch typically 1-3%); note USDA carbohydrate-by-difference overstates starch

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence_source: Soybean cultivar survey 82.3-213.6 mg/100g (SNI Global oxalate review); midpoint

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence_source: Kim et al. 2020 fppn doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1 (1H NMR, vegetable soybean)

components.name: molybdenum
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00271 / soy is a recognized high-molybdenum legume

components.name: gamma-tocopherol
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00271 / soy oil tocopherol profile (gamma is the dominant soy tocopherol)

components.name: delta-tocopherol (genuine soy tocopherol; no BiGG met)

oxygen_note: food substrate; O2 availability is a simulation condition, not a property of the food
basis: per 100 g (representative full-fat soy product / soy flour, non-fermented; USDA FDC 174273 as quantitative anchor)
